2ndxi.message: I won’t be able to play the game today as I am still sick sorry.
2ndxi.author: Henry Pomeroy

2ndxi.message:
Hey everyone, huge game today. We will meet at the changing rooms at 2:50. 

Let’s make sure we get the 3 points and if you are around and able, making your way over to the first 11 game to support them as well for a 6 point weekend. 

Thanks, see you all there 💪
2ndxi.author: Will Litchfield

2ndxi.message: Players, you are excused from class at 2.45.
2ndxi.author: Keita Elliott

2ndxi.message: Sorry I can’t make training my foot sore
2ndxi.author: Blayke Gallagher

2ndxi.message:
Training today is at the back field at 3:30. 

I’ll be there at 3:25 as I’ve got a quick talk with the Mr bueno. 

See you all there!
2ndxi.author: Will Litchfield

2ndxi.message: I won’t be able to make training today sorry, as I am sick
2ndxi.author: Henry Pomeroy

2ndxi.message: Kase is off sick and wont be at practice today.
2ndxi.author: Matt Stewary

2ndxi.message:
Hey guys, 

For anyone coming In the vans, we will meet outside the small gym at 2:20 aiming to leave by 2:30, arrive at St Peter’s just before 3:00pm for a 4pm ko. 


I’m staying around to help with the 1st 11 game so anyone coming back in the vans will also have to hang around until after the first 11 game or get a ride back with parents or someone else. 

Any questions or concerns message me on here directly.  

Cheers
2ndxi.author: Will Litchfield

2ndxi.message: What time do they need to be at the field? I’ll bring Laiko directly
2ndxi.author: Julie Laiko’s mum

2ndxi.message: Will there be a school van for this Friday’s game or should players make their own way to St Peters?
2ndxi.author: Brian Lomas

2ndxi.message:
Hey guys, just a reminder about the first official training today after school. Be at the back field by 3:30, excited to see you all there. 

Thanks!
2ndxi.author: Will Litchfield

13a.message:
Great win today boys — 4–0 against Howick to start the season 🔴⚫
Asaf with two goals in the first half (21', 23') and Lui with two in the second (31', 33'). 2–0 at half time, 4–0 at full time.
18 shots on target, 9 corners, roughly 70% of the ball. Strong start — well done to everyone involved today 👏
Match report attached.
13a.author: Nick Mackeson-Smith

13a.message: Hi team. A few people have asked me where field 1 is. It’s at the back of the school down the hill. Here’s a map. See you tomorrow at 8am for warm up
13a.author: Nick Mackeson-Smith

13a.message: Hi guys sorry for the late reply, I will be there to help you get the gear
13a.author: Keita Elliott

13a.message: A question for Keita. I’m not sure of the match day set up yet, sorry
13a.author: Nick Mackeson-Smith

13a.message: Hey, tomorrow do I just open the container and get balls and cones for warmup or ?
13a.author: Mojtaba Sayed

13a.message: Does anyone have football boots I can borrow tomorrow size 7.5-9.5 us
13a.author: Pedro  Tonnelier-Rossi

13a.message:
Volunteers needed — Saturday’s game vs Howick

Two parents needed to help out please — one to referee a half, one to run the line.

Apologies for the short notice. I’ve only just picked up the manager role a few days out from the first game.

If you can help, please reply here. Cheers team.
13a.author: Nick Mackeson-Smith

13a.message:
This week’s game — Selwyn 13A Boys
vs Howick College
Saturday 2 May
Kick-off 9:00 AM
Selwyn College, Field 1 (HOME)
Arrive 8:00 AM for warm-up with Mojtaba

Players, please confirm your attendance in the calendar invitation as early as you can so Mojtaba can plan the squad. Parents - please don’t mark your attendance. Thanks team.
13a.author: Nick Mackeson-Smith

13a.message: You can count on me to show up
13a.author: Pedro  Tonnelier-Rossi

13a.message: Thanks Nick for your help! You should be able to edit the calendar now.
13a.author: Keita Elliott

13a.message: Keita - can you please give me access to create and update calendar events. Thanks.
13a.author: Nick Mackeson-Smith

13a.message:
Hi Mojtaba. Thanks for coaching this season. I’m looking forward to supporting you. Let me know if there’s anything you need as we go through the season, and I’ll do my best to help. 

I’m Harry’s dad. I’ve managed teams at Ellerslie AFC for a number of years, so pretty comfortable with what’s needed - just a little time poor, unfortunately. 

I’ll keep an eye on fixtures and drive for the boys to update their attendance once I have access to create events calendar events. Feel free to let me know if you need to adjust anything. 

I’m not sure I’ll be able to be at every game as I also have Ethan in the second XI and will be supporting his team too. If there’s a clash, I can only do one. Let’s see how the season goes. 

See you on Saturday morning. 

Cheers,
Nick
13a.author: Nick Mackeson-Smith

13a.message:
Welcome to Selwyn College 13A Boys Football 2026.

I’m Nick, managing the team this season alongside Mojtaba. A quick note on how things will run.

I’ll post fixture details at the start of each game week, a reminder the day before, and a call for parent volunteers to ref a half and run the line two days out — your help with this is hugely appreciated.

Fixtures can change mid-week. I’ll keep on top of updates and post changes as soon as I see them.

The biggest help — players, please update your TeamReach attendance early for training and matches. The earlier Mojtaba knows who’s in, the better he can plan sessions and teams.
Training is Tuesdays 7am on the Front Field. For matches please arrive one hour before kick-off.

Thanks in advance for your positive support this season.
Nick
13a.author: Nick Mackeson-Smith

13a.message: Hi team, I believe our game this week is against Howick at 9am at Selwyn. I would like the boys to be at selwyn at 8am for warmups. Thanks, see you all this Saturday!
13a.author: Mojtaba Sayed

13a.message: Hi Team, looking forward to the first game on Saturday. Mojtaba, what time would you like the boys there for warm-up?
13a.author: Pauline Howie

13a.message: Hello everyone, I am at selwyn right now but I can’t find anyone just wondering where training currently is. Thanks -Harry
13a.author: Harry Mackeson-Smith
